refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity is achieved through task execution, which depends on both vehicle capability and operator control modality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials over agreed duration; measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) against baseline and target; correlate with operator mode (direct vs. tele-operated), task type, and environmental conditions. Verify compliance with safety plan through incident/near-miss logs and safety manager sign-off.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time and payload capacity sufficient to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The on-site operator interface shall provide real-time control authority and situational awareness to sustain productivity_target during direct operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The tele-operation system shall provide remote operator with sufficient control fidelity and latency-tolerant feedback to sustain productivity_target during remote operation.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance, operator authentication) without degrading productivity below target.
FR-NGHE-001.5: The system shall provide productivity telemetry (cycle count, payload, duration, operator mode) to enable measurement and reporting of achieved productivity against target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-capacity vehicle with optimized hydraulic cycle and payload management; couple with dual-mode operator interface (direct + tele-operated) and real-time safety constraint enforcement; measure and report productivity metrics via telemetry.
DP-B: Deploy vehicle with baseline hydraulic cycle; rely on operator skill and manual safety compliance; measure productivity via manual logs.

DP-C: Implement vehicle with adaptive cycle optimization (AI-driven task sequencing); couple with predictive safety system and autonomous task execution; measure productivity via integrated telemetry.
DP-D: Provide vehicle with standard cycle; integrate tele-operation as optional add-on; implement safety as post-hoc monitoring; measure productivity via external audits.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate high-capacity vehicle with optimized hydraulic cycle and payload management; couple with dual-mode operator interface (direct + tele-operated) and real-time safety constraint enforcement; measure and report productivity metrics via telemetry.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle optimization is independent of operator mode selection (direct vs. tele-operated); both modes can leverage the same hydraulic performance.
Safety constraint enforcement (geofencing, speed limits) is decoupled from productivity measurement; constraints are applied uniformly regardless of operator mode.
Telemetry collection is a passive observation layer; it does not couple with vehicle control or operator interface logic.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because each sub-component (vehicle capability, operator interface modality, safety enforcement, telemetry) can be designed and tested independently. The integration points are well-defined: operator commands → vehicle control, safety constraints → command filtering, telemetry → data collection. No circular dependencies or hidden feedback loops detected.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Productivity_target is achievable with conventional hydraulic cycle optimization; no exotic propulsion or autonomous task planning required.
ASSUMPTION: Dual-mode operator interface (direct + tele-operated) can be implemented with acceptable latency and control fidelity for both modes.
ASSUMPTION: Real-time safety constraint enforcement does not introduce unacceptable latency or command rejection that would degrade productivity below target.
RISK: If productivity_target is very aggressive (e.g., >20% above industry baseline), DP-A may be insufficient; DP-C (autonomous optimization) may be required.
RISK: Tele-operation latency may exceed operator tolerance, reducing effective productivity in remote mode; mitigation requires latency budget analysis and predictive control algorithms.

RISK: Safety constraint enforcement may conflict with productivity optimization (e.g., speed limits reduce cycle time); mitigation requires careful tuning and stakeholder agreement on safety-productivity trade-offs.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Retain DP-A as baseline. Conduct latency budget analysis for tele-operation and safety constraint enforcement. If latency exceeds operator tolerance (>500 ms round-trip), escalate to DP-C (autonomous optimization with predictive control). If productivity_target cannot be met with DP-A, escalate to DP-C. Document assumptions and trade-offs in design rationale.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. It owns the overall productivity delivery, operator mode selection logic, and end-to-end telemetry aggregation. The NGHE Solution is responsible for ensuring that the integrated system achieves productivity_target while maintaining safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task definitions (dig, load, transport, dump) and productivity targets from Site Manager or task planning system. Includes task parameters (location, volume, payload type) and success criteria.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs productivity metrics (cycle count, payload, duration, operator mode, task completion status) to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality for monitoring and reporting. Includes real-time and historical data.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outputs safety alerts and constraint violations (geofence breach, speed limit exceeded, collision risk, operator authentication failure) to Safety Manager and on-site personnel. Includes severity levels and recommended actions.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides communication channel for tele-operator commands and vehicle telemetry in remote operation mode. Includes latency and bandwidth constraints.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for productivity delivery. It owns the integration logic, operator mode selection, and end-to-end telemetry. Minimal diffusion: only the Network Link interface is partially external (shared with Teleoperation module for remote operation).

Vehicle owns the hydraulic cycle optimization and payload management, which are core to productivity. However, Vehicle does not own operator interface, safety enforcement, or telemetry aggregation. Significant diffusion: productivity depends on Vehicle capability, but also on operator control and safety constraints, which are external to Vehicle.

Teleoperation owns the remote operator interface and command transmission, which are necessary for tele-operated productivity. However, Teleoperation does not own vehicle capability, safety enforcement, or telemetry aggregation. High diffusion: productivity in remote mode depends on Teleoperation, but also on Vehicle and Safety Services.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance), which affects productivity. However, Safety Services does not own vehicle capability, operator interface, or telemetry. High diffusion: safety constraints are applied across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, and productivity measurement is external.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for FR-NGHE-001 because it owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and is responsible for ensuring that the combined system achieves productivity_target. The refined FR sub-components (vehicle capability, operator interface, safety enforcement, telemetry) are distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules, but the overall productivity delivery is owned by NGHE Solution.
containment_risks: RISK: Productivity measurement (FR-NGHE-001.5) may be diffused across Vehicle (cycle time), Teleoperation (remote mode latency), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement overhead). Mitigation: NGHE Solution must own the telemetry aggregation and reporting logic to ensure consistent measurement.

RISK: Operator mode selection logic (direct vs. tele-operated) is not explicitly assigned to any module. Mitigation: NGHE Solution must own this logic and provide clear interfaces to Vehicle and Teleoperation for mode switching.

RISK: Safety-productivity trade-offs (e.g., speed limits reducing cycle time) may not be visible at NGHE Solution level. Mitigation: NGHE Solution must own the trade-off analysis and provide configuration parameters to Safety Services for tuning.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution with explicit sub-allocations to Vehicle (FR-NGHE-001.1), on-site operator interface (FR-NGHE-001.2), Teleoperation (FR-NGHE-001.3), Safety Services (FR-NGHE-001.4), and NGHE Solution (FR-NGHE-001.5 telemetry). Define clear interfaces between modules for operator mode selection, safety constraint parameters, and telemetry aggregation. Establish ownership of safety-productivity trade-off analysis at NGHE Solution level.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (integrated vehicle capability, dual-mode operator interface, real-time safety enforcement, telemetry) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve productivity_target while maintaining safety compliance). The DP is sufficiently detailed to guide design of sub-components (vehicle cycle, operator interface, safety services, telemetry). No circular dependencies or hidden assumptions detected. Coupling check passes.

diffusion_risks: Productivity measurement is distributed across Vehicle (cycle time), Teleoperation (remote mode latency), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement overhead). Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns telemetry aggregation and reporting.
Operator mode selection logic is not explicitly assigned. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns mode selection and provides interfaces to Vehicle and Teleoperation.
Safety-productivity trade-offs are not visible at NGHE Solution level. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns trade-off analysis and provides configuration parameters to Safety Services.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate top-level container (containment_score 95). Sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are well-defined modules with clear ownership boundaries. Interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and traceable to stakeholders (Site Manager, Maintenance/Quality, Safety Manager, Tele-operator).

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trials. Productivity measurement (m³/h or ton/h) is directly observable and measurable. Safety compliance is verifiable via incident logs and safety manager sign-off. Operator mode (direct vs. tele-operated) is selectable and testable. Latency and control fidelity in tele-operation mode are measurable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution with clear sub-allocations to Vehicle (cycle capability), on-site operator interface (direct control), Teleoperation (remote control), Safety Services (constraint enforcement), and NGHE Solution (telemetry and mode selection). Each sub-allocation is traceable to a specific module and interface. Acceptance criteria (productivity_target, safety compliance) are clear and measurable.

recommended_revisions: Define latency budget for tele-operation mode (e.g., <500 ms round-trip) and include in DP and IM specifications.
Define safety-productivity trade-off parameters (e.g., speed limit reduction, geofence margin) and include in Safety Services configuration.
Define telemetry aggregation logic (e.g., cycle count, payload, duration, operator mode) and include in NGHE Solution specification.
Define operator mode selection logic (e.g., manual selection, automatic failover) and include in NGHE Solution specification.
Establish acceptance criteria for each sub-FR (e.g., vehicle cycle time, operator interface response time, safety constraint latency, telemetry accuracy) and include in verification plan.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that has been successfully refined into five atomic sub-FRs (vehicle capability, on-site operator interface, tele-operation, safety enforcement, telemetry). DP-A is a coherent design approach that addresses all sub-FRs without excessive coupling. NGHE Solution is the appropriate top-level IM boundary with clear sub-allocations to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. All gates pass. The next level of decomposition should focus on the five sub-FRs to define detailed DPs and IMs for each sub-component. This is a natural decomposition point where the system architecture becomes visible and module boundaries are clear.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time and payload capacity sufficient to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The on-site operator interface shall provide real-time control authority and situational awareness to sustain productivity_target during direct operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The tele-operation system shall provide remote operator with sufficient control fidelity and latency-tolerant feedback to sustain productivity_target during remote operation.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance, operator authentication) without degrading productivity below target.
FR-NGHE-001.5: The system shall provide productivity telemetry (cycle count, payload, duration, operator mode) to enable measurement and reporting of achieved productivity against target.

assumptions: Productivity_target is achievable with conventional hydraulic cycle optimization; no exotic propulsion or autonomous task planning required.
Dual-mode operator interface (direct + tele-operated) can be implemented with acceptable latency and control fidelity for both modes.
Real-time safety constraint enforcement does not introduce unacceptable latency or command rejection that would degrade productivity below target.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and can be translated into enforceable constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance, operator authentication).
Telemetry collection and reporting can be implemented with sufficient accuracy and timeliness to support productivity measurement and reporting.
open_risks: RISK: If productivity_target is very aggressive (e.g., >20% above industry baseline), DP-A may be insufficient; DP-C (autonomous optimization) may be required. Mitigation: Conduct feasibility study and latency budget analysis.

RISK: Tele-operation latency may exceed operator tolerance, reducing effective productivity in remote mode. Mitigation: Define latency budget (<500 ms round-trip) and implement predictive control algorithms.

RISK: Safety constraint enforcement may conflict with productivity optimization (e.g., speed limits reduce cycle time). Mitigation: Conduct trade-off analysis and establish safety-productivity parameters.
RISK: Operator mode selection logic may not be clear or may introduce delays. Mitigation: Define mode selection logic (manual vs. automatic) and include in NGHE Solution specification.
RISK: Telemetry aggregation may introduce latency or data loss. Mitigation: Define telemetry architecture and include in NGHE Solution specification.
evidence_needed: Productivity_target value and basis (industry baseline, customer requirement, contractual commitment).
Worksite safety plan and operational policies (geofencing zones, speed limits, collision avoidance rules, operator authentication requirements).
Vehicle specifications (hydraulic cycle time, payload capacity, fuel consumption, maintenance intervals).
Operator interface specifications (direct control: joystick, display, feedback; tele-operated: latency budget, control fidelity, situational awareness).
Safety services specifications (constraint enforcement logic, alert generation, override procedures).
Telemetry specifications (data points, collection frequency, reporting format, accuracy requirements).
Representative operations trial plan (task types, duration, environmental conditions, measurement methodology).
Acceptance criteria for each sub-FR (vehicle cycle time, operator interface response time, safety constraint latency, telemetry accuracy).
